# Term Explanation Guide

## Goal
Explain technical terms so a first-time reader can keep going without external lookup.

## Rule of First Mention
When a technical term first appears, add one short explanation in this pattern:
- What it is (plain language)
- Why it matters in this project

Example format:
- jiffies: a running tick counter used as the project's software clock, which schedules sampling, key scanning, and rendering.

## Recommended Terms to Explain
- bare-metal
- linker script
- toolchain
- polling
- interrupt
- jiffies
- debounce
- tare
- calibration
- counts/gram
- ring buffer
- exponential smoothing
- overload

## Writing Style
- One sentence per term at first mention.
- Avoid circular definitions and unexplained abbreviations.
- Prefer concrete effects over textbook wording.
- If an acronym is needed, write full name first, acronym second.

## Validation
- Ask: "Can a first-time reader follow this sentence without prior context?"
- If not, simplify and add one practical example.
